reasoning: The most recent TIPS 10-year yield is 2.01% (as of Mar 14, 2025), which resulted from a sharp two-week increase from 1.80%. The FRED tool’s statistical analysis provides an expected P(increase) of 30% for the near-term, driven by a broader negative linear trend over the last month and a half. Following instructions to use this statistical estimate as the primary anchor, the short-term probability is set near 0.32. Because longer-term bond yields behave closer to a random walk and long-term macro forecasting is imprecise, the uncertainty strongly increases over longer horizons. Thus, the probabilities gradually revert toward a 50% base rate as the forecast date extends to late 2025 and 2026.
